SEARCH
Package microsim

Class Main

java.lang.Object
microsim.Main
public class Main extends Object
Contains program entry point. Loads EPROM data and instantiates simulations based on them.

  • Method Details

    • greet

      private static void greet()
      Shows project name, version and authorship.

    • initInterfaces

      private static void initInterfaces(Simulation simulation)
      Initializes interfaces and attaches them to the given simulation. Handled interfaces are:
      1. Video window.
      2. Debug shell, always attached, activated if debug mode is requested (through argument).
      3. Keyboard: attached and listening from video window.
      Parameters:
      simulation - simulation instance to attach interfaces to

    • initSimulation

      public static Simulation initSimulation(MainEnvironment.SimulationInfo info)
      Instantiates a simulation. Initialization flow is:
      1. Instantiate a Simulation object with said configuration and data.
      2. Load EPROM data into simulation memory.
      3. Attach interfaces to simulation. These include VideoWindow, DebugShell, and keyboard source for the keyboard device. Attachment is handled by the initInterfaces(microsim.simulation.Simulation) method.
      Parameters:
      info - simulation info, with EPROM data to load into memory and name
      Returns:
      simulation that was instantiated

    • main

      public static void main(String[] args)
      Program entry point. Main program flow is:
      1. Use MainEnvironment to parse arguments and instantiate a list of simulation info objects.
      2. Instantiate these simulation info objects into actual simulations, attach interfaces to them, and execute them. Instantiation is handled by initSimulation(microsim.MainEnvironment.SimulationInfo).
      3. Begin simulations.
      Any simulation instantiation failure aborts the entire program. Return values for the program are:
      1. Normal termination.
      2. User error. Configuration and file I/O errors are considered user errors and return this code.
      3. Simulation error. These are issued when something actually breaks in the simulated environment.
      Parameters:
      args - program arguments
